user_intent_preservation.functional_behavior: Generate 3-phase PWM on GTM.TOM1 using existing pins P00.2–P00.7, center-aligned at 20 kHz. Initialize U/V/W duty at 25/50/75%. Every 500 ms increment each channel duty by +10% with wrap in 0–100% and clamp 100% to (period-1 tick). Complementary/DTM: currently not required; if later required, enable TOM1 CDTM/DTM while staying on IfxGtm_Pwm.
user_intent_preservation.technical_approach: Migrate from IfxGtm_Tom_PwmHl to unified IfxGtm_Pwm. Use TOM1 channels 5–10 mapped to P00.2–P00.7. Configure center-aligned 20 kHz with GTM CMU FXCLK (~100 MHz). Initialize three phases with requested duties and perform 500 ms duty steps using IfxGtm_Pwm_updateChannelsDutyImmediate. Keep DTM off now; if enabled later, manage dead-time via unified driver APIs.

driver_selection.selection_rationale: Unified high-level PWM driver supports multi-channel sync, center alignment, TOM submodule selection, and optional DTM. It is available in the template and matches TC3xx family and advanced 3-phase inverter needs.

runtime_function.name: GTM_TOM_3_Phase_Inverter_PWM_stepDuty
runtime_function.purpose: Every 500 ms, add +10% to each U/V/W duty, wrap within 0–100%, and when 100% clamp duty to (period-1 tick) before updating all six channels atomically.

api_changes.reason: Unified API for synchronized multi-channel duty update
Mode handled at initialization in unified driver

migration_notes.context_preserved: The solution remains on GTM.TOM1 with the exact pins (P00.2–P00.7), runs center-aligned at 20 kHz using FXCLK, initializes U/V/W at 25/50/75%, and steps duty every 500 ms using the specified immediate multi-channel update API. Dead-time is omitted now per requirement; if complementary outputs are later needed, retain IfxGtm_Pwm and enable TOM1 DTM with IfxGtm_Pwm_updateChannelsDeadTimeImmediate (and GTM DTM configuration) without changing the driver.
